header.logoutError: Could not log out. Please try again.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: An unexpected error occurred. You can try reloading the page or restarting.

debts.avalancheDescription: Pay the highest interest rate debt first. Saves more money on interest.
debts.snowballDescription: Pay the smallest debt first. Builds psychological motivation faster.
debts.noDebtsDescription: You have total financial freedom in spending.
debts.name: Name

goals.sequentialDescription: Save for one goal at a time by priority. Faster per goal.
goals.parallelDescription: Save for all goals simultaneously. Progress on all at once.
goals.waitingDescription: Goals start accumulating on {{startDate}} — when you finish paying off your debts ({{debtFreeDate}}). Prioritize eliminating high-cost debts first.

biweekly.allTasksDone: All tasks for this period are done
biweekly.phaseObjective: In this phase, the biweekly goal is: {{objective}}

emergency.tip1: High-yield savings account (separate from daily spending)
emergency.tip2: A separate account from your everyday use — hard to spend impulsively
emergency.tip3: Not in stocks or high-risk investments — it must be readily available
emergency.tip4: Keep at least 1 month always, even while paying off debt

insights.title: Financial Tutor Analysis
insights.subtitle: Based on your current situation, here is my complete diagnosis and the most effective action plan for your case.
insights.noAlertsDescription: Your financial situation is in good shape.

settings.logoutError: Error logging out. Please try again.
settings.name: Name
settings.redoOnboardingDesc: Edit your initial data while keeping history
settings.resetLocalDesc: Clear local cache. Your cloud data will reload when you sign back in.
settings.deleteAccountDesc: Delete your account and all associated data. This is irreversible.
settings.resetWarning: This clears your browser's local data. Your cloud data will not be affected and will reload automatically.
settings.deleteWarning: Your account, all financial data, income, expenses, debts, goals, and transactions will be deleted. This cannot be undone.
settings.deleteError: Error deleting account. Please try again.

auth.invalidCredentials: Invalid credentials. Check your email and password.
auth.startControlling: Start taking control of your finances
auth.name: Name
auth.passwordMinError: Password must be at least 6 characters.
auth.confirmationSent: We sent a confirmation link to {{email}}. Click the link to activate your account.
auth.recoverDescription: We'll send you a link to reset your password
auth.linkSentDescription: If an account exists for {{email}}, you'll receive a link to reset your password.
auth.passwordUpdatedDesc: Your password has been updated successfully.
auth.loginSubtext: Dobby gives you a clear, personalized roadmap to get out of debt, build your emergency fund, and reach your financial goals.
auth.signupSubtext: Set up your income, expenses, and goals in minutes. Dobby builds a personalized plan to get you where you want to be.

steps.incomeDesc: How much and when you get paid

income.title: How much do you earn?
income.subtitle: Set your currency and income sources
income.name: Name
income.netAmount: This amount already has deductions applied (net)
income.minOneIncome: Add at least one income to continue

expenses.title: What are your fixed expenses?
expenses.subtitle: Add all your recurring monthly expenses
expenses.name: Name

goals.title: What do you want to achieve?
goals.subtitle: Define your financial goals — the app prioritizes them automatically
goals.emergencyFundDesc: How much money do you have saved as a reserve?

diagnosis.criticalExpenseMsg: Your expenses represent {{ratio}}% of your income. The recommended maximum is 80%.
diagnosis.criticalExpenseAction: Review non-essential expenses in the Budget section
diagnosis.highExpensesMsg: You spend {{ratio}}% of your income. Try to get below 75%.
diagnosis.highExpensesAction: Identify which categories you can reduce
diagnosis.noEmergencyFundMsg: You have no emergency fund. An unexpected event can seriously affect your finances.
diagnosis.lowEmergencyFundMsg: You only have {{months}} months of expenses as an emergency fund.
diagnosis.buildEmergencyAction: Build an emergency fund of at least 3 months
diagnosis.insufficientFundMsg: You have {{months}} months covered. 3-6 months is recommended.
diagnosis.creditUtilizationMsg: You're using {{percent}}% of your credit card limit. This affects your credit history.
diagnosis.creditUtilizationAction: Prioritize paying off your card balances
diagnosis.highCostDebtMsg: You have {{count}} debt(s) with rates above 1.5%/mo. They cost you {{amount}}/mo in interest.
diagnosis.highCostDebtAction: Activate the aggressive payment plan to eliminate them first
diagnosis.negativeFlowMsg: Your expenses and debts exceed your income by {{amount}}/mo.
diagnosis.negativeFlowAction: You need to reduce expenses or increase income urgently
diagnosis.excellentSavingsMsg: You're saving {{rate}}% of your income. Consider investing the surplus.
diagnosis.strengthExpenses: You control your expenses well — below 65% of income
diagnosis.strengthFund: You have {{months}} months of emergency fund
diagnosis.strengthNoDebt: You have no debts — total financial freedom in spending
diagnosis.strengthCreditLow: You use less than 30% of your credit card limit
diagnosis.strengthFreeFlow: You have a healthy free flow to build wealth
diagnosis.weakExpenseRatio: Expense/income ratio at {{ratio}}% — 5+ points over the maximum
diagnosis.weakFund: Emergency fund: only {{months}} of 3 recommended months
diagnosis.weakLowFreeFlow: Low free flow for emergencies and goals
diagnosis.recEliminateDesc: This debt has a rate of {{rate}}%/mo and costs you {{interest}}/mo in interest alone.
diagnosis.recEliminateImpact: Paying it off in {{months}} months saves {{savings}} in interest
diagnosis.recEliminateStep1: Activate the avalanche plan in the Debts section
diagnosis.recEliminateStep2: Allocate all your free flow ({{freeFlow}}) to this debt
diagnosis.recEliminateStep3: Temporarily suspend other savings until it's paid off
diagnosis.recBuildFundDesc: You need {{amount}} more to have 3 months covered.
diagnosis.recBuildFundImpact: In {{months}} months you'll have 3 months of financial backup
diagnosis.recBuildFundStep1: Open a savings account dedicated only to the emergency fund
diagnosis.recBuildFundStep3: Don't touch this money except for real emergencies
diagnosis.recReviewExpensesDesc: You have {{amount}}/mo in non-essential fixed expenses (gym, subscriptions, etc.).
diagnosis.recReviewExpensesImpact: Reducing by 30% frees up {{amount}}/mo for priority goals
diagnosis.recReviewExpenseStep: Evaluate whether you can reduce or eliminate: {{name}} ({{amount}}/mo)

phases.eliminateHighCostDesc: Pay off {{count}} debt(s) with rates above 1.5%/mo using the avalanche method.
phases.objPayMinimum: Pay the minimum on all debts
phases.objFreeFlowToDebt: Allocate all free flow to the most expensive debt
phases.objNoNewDebt: Do not take on new debt
phases.liquidateRemainingDesc: Finish paying moderate-interest debts while building your financial foundation.
phases.objStartEmergency20: Start emergency fund with 20% of free flow
phases.buildEmergencyDesc: Accumulate {{amount}} (3 months of essential expenses) to protect against emergencies.
phases.objSave40to50: Save 40-50% of free flow in a separate account
phases.objDontTouch: Don't use this money except for real emergencies
phases.reachGoalsDesc: Save for your {{count}} goal(s) in priority order.
phases.freedomInvestmentDesc: Debt-free, with emergency fund and goals reached. Start investing for the future.
phases.objInvest30to40: Invest 30-40% of income in investment instruments
phases.objPlanRetirement: Plan early retirement or financial independence
